events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess 4-gas monitor readings: O2 20.9 %VOL, LEL 2%, H2S 55 ppm, CO 5 ppm. Determine if entry can be authorized for permit-required confined space. Need identify any reading outside acceptable entry limits. Need apply OSHA 1910.146. Need know typical acceptable entry limits: O2: 19.5-23.5% (or 22%? OSHA defines oxygen content in permit space atmosphere between 19.5% and 23.5%). LEL: must be less than 10% of LEL (some standards <10% LEL for entry). H2S: OSHA PEL 10 ppm TWA, ceiling 15 ppm, IDLH 100 ppm. For permit space, acceptable entry conditions: no hazardous atmosphere; if toxic substances present, concentration must be below PEL or other published exposure level. H2S 55 ppm exceeds ceiling and STEL (15 ppm ceiling, 10 ppm 8-hr TWA), but below IDLH 100 ppm. It is hazardous atmosphere. CO: PEL 50 ppm TWA, ceiling? OSHA PEL 50 ppm; 5 ppm acceptable. O2 20.9 acceptable. LEL 2% (<10% LEL) acceptable. H2S 55 ppm unacceptable. Therefore decision fail, risk high (H2S toxic, > PEL but < IDLH). Work_stage planned. component_conditions none. percent_complete maybe 0 (pre-entry test). value maybe 55 (the out-of-limit reading). findings: H2S concentration 55 ppm exceeds acceptable entry limit (15 ppm ceiling/10 ppm TWA). actions: ventilate and retest, do not authorize entry until H2S below acceptable limits, ensure respiratory protection if needed, investigate source. references: OSHA 1910.146(b), (c)(5)(ii)(B), (d)(5)(ii). Need produce JSON with all fields. Need ensure valid JSON.

H2S at 55 ppm exceeds OSHA exposure limits (10 ppm TWA / 15 ppm ceiling), creating a hazardous atmosphere and prohibiting entry until corrected.",

  ]
